attached_docs: KFM Markdown Modernization & GitHub Documentation Implementation Agent v4.0.0
Kansas Frontier Matrix — AI Build Operating Contract.md

validation_gates.reason: Repository identity, push permission, current base, target blob, Directory Rules, pipeline child and compatibility lanes, pipeline_specs, tests, receipt schema, workflows, CODEOWNERS, and generated-receipt lane were inspected.
The complete v0.2 target was read before replacement, including metadata, all headings, tables, commands, lane map, open questions, and final maintainer note.

The review branch was created from main@19670ca8e2c8a709fc69cd41173851f8359c8281; pipelines/README.md remained blob 9fb38acf5a67ca43608617d73a273d06f5f84db5 before mutation; no overlapping open pull request or matching modernization branch was found.

The canonical pipelines root README was updated in place and the generated receipt uses the existing data/receipts/generated/ process-memory lane. No root, pipeline lane, spec authority, lifecycle phase, policy, schema, proof, release, or public path was created or promoted.
The README was reconciled against current shared, domain, proof, compatibility, cross-lane, biodiversity, pipeline-spec, test, receipt, workflow, package, and ADR evidence at the pinned base.

The first twelve H2 sections exactly match Purpose, Authority level, Status, What belongs here, What does NOT belong here, Inputs, Outputs, Validation, Review burden, Related folders, ADRs, and Last reviewed.

UTF-8, LF endings, final newline, KFM metadata, one H1, balanced language-tagged fences, Mermaid declaration, Markdown parsing, HTML balance, image alt text, and file-size limits were checked; 35 of 35 local checks passed.

Thirteen v0.2 fragment identities were retained explicitly, including pipelines, numbered purpose/authority/lifecycle/anti-collapse/belongs/exclusions/lane-map/inputs/gates/expansion/done/open anchors.
Thirty-eight internal fragment references resolve, fourteen custom IDs are unique, and seventy-four repository-relative links passed path-safety review.
Six descriptive static Shields badges link to evidence-bearing sections. No dynamic CI, release, coverage, registry, or maturity feed was added.
Badges communicate static document posture only; live endpoint reachability was not required or claimed.

Stable identity, executable-versus-declarative split, lifecycle invariant, non-publication rule, anti-collapse rules, placement guidance, lane map, inputs/outputs, gates, expansion rules, definition of done, open questions, maintainer note, and legacy fragments were preserved or strengthened.
No private key, common token pattern, credential, private endpoint, restricted payload, protected coordinate, living-person record, genomic material, or hidden reasoning was introduced.

GitHub returned blob c2bee1db957a665b973b44aea8bda63bdd82b7e5, matching the locally computed Git blob for the validated 64,991-byte README; the content SHA-256 is 33748512339f145f74fa4f4102e0b4a4bc28d3e397e17a8b9b56c12f0517a5cf.

Required fields, artifact/hash closure, truth labels, validation outcomes, citations, model metadata, timestamp, and pending human-review state were checked against the inspected generated-receipt schema.

Documentation/provenance-only change; no pipeline, source activation, spec, contract, schema, policy, fixture, test, workflow, lifecycle record, receipt implementation, release object, deployment, runtime, or public behavior changed.
Repository checks run after draft pull-request creation and were unavailable at receipt emission time; no CI pass or merge-readiness claim is made.

notes: AI-authored documentation-only modernization on a scoped review branch. pipelines/README.md is upgraded in place to v0.3, preserves legacy fragment identities, and remains subordinate to source admission, lifecycle, evidence, policy, release, correction, rollback, and public-client governance. The generated receipt records process provenance only; it is not pipeline evidence, human approval, proof, release authority, lifecycle promotion, or KFM publication. Human review remains pending.
